meta.project: ILM — Interactive Learning Material (V1, workflow v2)
meta.pipeline_step: Assemble + schema validate + human gate + render
meta.diagram_node: Assemble Concept Unit -> Human review gate -> Output
meta.note: Split from ilm_eval_set.json v0.1.0; cases unchanged.

source_spans.S1: An AI agent is a program that uses a language model to decide which actions to take. It runs in a loop: observe the current state, choose a tool, execute it, and feed the result back into the model. The loop ends when the model decides the task is complete.
source_spans.S2: Tool calling lets the model request that the host program run a specific function, such as a web search or a calculator, and return the result to the model.
source_spans.S3: A system prompt is a fixed instruction given to the model before the conversation starts. It sets the agent's role, rules, and output format.
source_spans.S4: Retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) fetches relevant passages from a document store and adds them to the prompt, so the model can answer using information it was never trained on. The model's weights are not changed.
source_spans.S5: A human-in-the-loop gate requires a person to approve the agent's output before it takes effect.

cases.edge_case: source_span is whitespace-only, not empty string
cases.failure_mode: `if source_span:` passes on whitespace. Grader must check source_span.strip() != ''.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: flag if source_span is None or source_span.strip() is empty.

cases.edge_case: Required field present but null — key-existence checks pass, type validation must fail
cases.failure_mode: 'mini_quiz' in unit is True; the value is null. pydantic in models.py catches this only if the field is typed non-Optional — this case enforces that typing decision.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: validate against the pydantic model; required components must be non-null and non-empty, not merely present as keys.

cases.edge_case: scenarios component present but is an empty list
cases.failure_mode: Empty list is truthy enough for key checks and schema-valid if typed list — but zero scenarios means the component is effectively missing. Require min length on list components.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: flag if any list-typed component has length 0.

cases.edge_case: review.status approved but approved_by is empty — approval recorded without an approver
cases.failure_mode: Checking status == 'approved' alone passes this. A status that flipped without an approver identity/timestamp is exactly how an auto-retry loop accidentally self-approves. This protects PRD non-negotiable #1.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: rendered units must have review.status == 'approved' AND non-empty approved_by AND non-null approved_at.

cases.edge_case: Rendered concept passed partition at generation time, but a past-RM mapping was added later — staleness edge
cases.failure_mode: 'prompting basics' is in both lists — the duplicate check must run at render time against the CURRENT past-concepts registry, not rely on a partition verdict cached from generation time.
cases.judge_instructions: Code grader: at render time, flag any rendered concept whose normalized title appears in the current past-concepts registry.

cases.edge_case: Fully valid unit — calibrates code graders against false positives

candidate_unit_fields.title: Human-in-the-loop approval gate
candidate_unit_fields.explanation: A human-in-the-loop gate means a person must approve the agent's output before it takes effect. Nothing reaches the end user without that approval. It is a safety check, not a performance feature.
candidate_unit_fields.analogy: It's like an editor approving an article before printing: the writer is the agent, the editor's sign-off is the gate.
scenarios: A bank's email agent drafts complaint responses; a support lead approves each draft before it is sent.
candidate_unit_fields.source_span: A human-in-the-loop gate requires a person to approve the agent's output before it takes effect.

cases.judge_instructions: All fields present, typed, non-empty; review fully recorded; concept not in past registry. Any grader flagging this unit has a false-positive bug.
